---
title: "MetricForce"
generated: true
description: "Generic force from any [`Metric`](../nitrix-register/Metric.qmd) -- the escape hatch."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.register`](../nitrix-register.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.register._force`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:MetricForce -->
```python
class MetricForce
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:MetricForce -->
Generic force from any [`Metric`](../nitrix-register/Metric.qmd) -- the escape hatch.

Drives a dense-field recipe by $-\partial(\text{metric.cost}) /
\partial\,\text{warped} \cdot \nabla\,\text{warped}$ (the cost gradient via
`jax.grad`), so a cross-modal metric (mutual information, correlation
ratio) or a custom descriptor can register diffeomorphically with **no**
hand-written force.  *No performance guarantee* -- the autodiff cost is the
price of generality; ship a closed-form [`Force`](../nitrix-register/Force.qmd) for a metric that
earns one.

**Two magnitude regimes, keyed on** `metric.is_spatial_mean`:

- **Spatial-mean cost** (sum-of-squared-differences or local
  cross-correlation): the cost reduces by a per-voxel mean, so rescaling the
  gradient by the voxel count undoes the $1/N$ and recovers the
  *sum-convention* gradient the closed forms use -- [`MetricForce`](../nitrix-register/MetricForce.qmd)
  wrapping a local-CC metric is then numerically identical to the closed
  [`LNCCForce`](../nitrix-register/LNCCForce.qmd) (the parity oracle, in magnitude not only direction),
  and the magnitude shrinks naturally as the gradient does.  The
  sum-of-squared-differences metric has **no** normalised closed form here
  -- wrapping it gives the *raw* optical-flow gradient $(\text{warped}
  - \text{fixed}) \cdot \nabla\,\text{warped}$, not the Thirion-normalised
  Demons force, and recovers markedly worse on a monomodal warp (it is
  unscaled by $|\nabla|^2 + (m - f)^2$, so the regulariser or
  trust-region clamp dominates and the field under-recovers); for monomodal
  SSD-type registration prefer [`DemonsForce`](../nitrix-register/DemonsForce.qmd) (the diffeomorphic
  Demons recipe default).
- **Global histogram scalar** (mutual information, correlation ratio, or any
  metric that does not declare `is_spatial_mean`): there is no $1/N$
  to undo, so multiplying by the voxel count would be an arbitrary,
  metric-scale-dependent constant -- under the **unclamped** Demons driver
  (`step=None`) that feeds a magnitude no one has tuned straight into the
  velocity.  Instead the force is normalised to a target per-voxel RMS
  `magnitude` (via `_normalise_rms`), a controlled,
  metric-scale-invariant step.  Under the clamped SyN driver the subsequent
  trust-region clamp only sharpens this; the direction (the parity that
  matters for a metric with no closed form) is untouched either way.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:MetricForce.bind -->
```python
bind(fixed: Float[Array, '*spatial'], *, ndim: int, rel_spacing: RelSpacing = None) -> _BoundMetric
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Bind to `fixed`, carrying the metric and magnitude settings.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:MetricForce.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(metric: Metric, magnitude: float = 0.5) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:MetricForce -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
